question: Compare the NuScale design life from Ch.01 with the RPV embrittlement projections in Ch.05.

nuscale_ch01_0049.title: Central Utility Building
nuscale_ch01_0049.summary: The Central Utility Building (CUB) is a nonsafety-related structure that houses essential utility services for the plant, such as chiller equipment, air systems, water treatment, maintenance, and security functions. It supports plant operations but does not contain safety-related systems. The design of the NuScale Power Plant also emphasizes minimizing human error through automation, digital controls, and effective workload management, as detailed in the Human Factors Engineering (HFE) program.

nuscale_ch05_0016.title: Pressure-Temperature Limits, Pressurized Thermal Shock, and Charpy Upper-Shelf Energy Data and Analyses
nuscale_ch05_0016.summary: This section outlines the basis for establishing operational pressure and temperature limits for the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) to comply with regulatory requirements over the plant’s 60-year lifespan. It describes the use of ASME and NRC methodologies to calculate pressure-temperature limit curves, accounting for vessel embrittlement, and provides figures and tables with these limits to ensure protection against non-ductile failure.

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the requirements and procedures for inspection, shipment, installation, operating conditions, inservice surveillance, and threaded fasteners for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards. It specifies the use of nondestructive examinations, strict packaging and environmental controls during shipment, and adherence to quality assurance protocols. Key references include the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code, ASME NQA-1 quality assurance standards, and ASTM practices for material testing.

nuscale_ch01_0050.title: Diesel Generator Buildings
nuscale_ch01_0050.summary: The Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs) in the NuScale Power Plant each house a single backup diesel generator and associated auxiliary equipment, but do not contain any safety-related systems or support engineered safety functions (ESFs). Their primary purpose is to provide support and housing for the generators, including related systems such as HVAC and maintenance areas.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module's reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a key pressure boundary to prevent the release of fission products and provides support for various reactor components, with all materials and fabrication processes adhering to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure long-term integrity.

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV) is a key component that houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer, serving as a primary barrier to fission product release and supporting various reactor systems. The RPV and its components are constructed from materials specified by the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code to ensure structural integrity and safety throughout the plant’s design life.

nuscale_ch05_0010.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Materials
nuscale_ch05_0010.summary: The Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary (RCPB) materials, including welds, are selected and fabricated in accordance with ASME BPVC standards and comply with relevant U.S. regulatory requirements to ensure high quality, environmental compatibility, and fracture resistance. These materials are subject to strict quality assurance measures and testing to meet fracture toughness criteria, with detailed specifications provided in referenced tables.

nuscale_ch01_0419.title: DSRS 10.2.3, Rev 0: Turbine Rotor IntegrityII.2 Fracture Toughness
nuscale_ch01_0419.summary: This section explains that the NuScale plant design uses physical barriers to protect safety-related structures, systems, and components (SSCs) from turbine missile hazards. As a result, requirements related to turbine rotor integrity, including fracture toughness and associated inspections, are not applicable because the plant does not rely on turbine missile generation probabilities for safety.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: The steam generators in the NuScale Power Module are integrated within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which also houses other key components such as the pressurizer, safety valves, and emergency cooling systems. The steam generator system includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and access ports, with the RPV itself forming the outer pressure boundary for the steam generators and contributing to the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch01_0430.title: SRP 10.3.6, Rev 3: Steam and Feedwater System MaterialsII.2 Fracture Toughness of Class 2 and 3 Components
nuscale_ch01_0430.summary: This section states that the NuScale design does not contain any Class 2 or 3 components, so the fracture toughness requirements of SRP 10.3.6, Rev 3 are not applicable. The document also summarizes conformance or departures from other regulatory criteria for various steam and feedwater system components, noting where requirements are met, not applicable, or where NuScale-specific criteria are used instead.

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: This section outlines the procedures and regulatory compliance measures to ensure the integrity of the NuScale reactor vessel over its 60-year design life. It details the development of operating procedures to maintain pressure-temperature limits, evaluates the vessel’s resistance to pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy degradation, and confirms that predicted material properties remain within safety criteria throughout the vessel’s operational lifespan.

nuscale_ch01_0484.title: DSRS 11.3, Rev 0: Gaseous Waste Management SystemII.10 Relevant RGs, ISG, and BTP
nuscale_ch01_0484.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with regulatory criteria for the Gaseous Waste Management System (DSRS 11.3, Rev 0). Most criteria are met, with partial conformance noted for dose design objectives and features to minimize contamination, where site-specific responsibilities fall to the COL applicant. The system otherwise conforms to requirements for design, seismic classification, filtration, automatic controls, and safety against hydrogen explosions and leakage.

nuscale_ch01_0477.title: DSRS 11.3, Rev 0: Gaseous Waste Management SystemII.3 Seismic Design and Quality Group Classification of Components and Structures Housing Gaseous Waste Management System
nuscale_ch01_0477.summary: This section confirms that the seismic design and quality group classification of components and structures housing the Gaseous Waste Management System fully conforms to regulatory requirements, with no exceptions noted. The NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report demonstrates compliance with DSRS 11.3, Rev 0, ensuring that these systems are appropriately designed to withstand seismic events.

nuscale_ch05_0021.title: Reactor Coolant System Piping
nuscale_ch05_0021.summary: The section discusses the design and degradation assessment of the NuScale Reactor Coolant System's steam generator (SG) tubing. It notes that the primary degradation observed in similar operating reactors is wear from flow-induced vibration or foreign objects, and that NuScale's design—with its helical tubing and novel support structure—does not increase risk compared to existing designs. Prototypic testing and standard SG program guidelines are deemed sufficient, with wear identified as the most likely degradation mechanism and corrosion risks assessed as low.

edges.description: Section A discusses compliance with ASME BPVC requirements for RPV welds and attachments, while Section B outlines inspection and handling requirements per ASME BPVC and NQA-1, both providing details on how the RPV's fabrication and maintenance processes adhere to regulatory codes that underpin the vessel's projected embrittlement performance and design life.

Section A provides detailed analyses and compliance information regarding the reactor pressure vessel's (RPV) material properties and inspection requirements, which are necessary to assess the RPV's embrittlement projections over the design life described in Section B.

Section A provides detailed analyses and compliance information regarding the reactor pressure vessel's (RPV) material properties and inspection requirements, which are specific technical details that support the general description of the RPV materials and configuration outlined in Section B.

Section A details the inspection, shipment, and storage requirements for the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which are necessary to ensure the RPV's integrity and longevity, directly supporting the design life considerations discussed in Section B's general description of the RPV as a critical pressure-retaining component.

Section A details the inspection, handling, and storage requirements for the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) to ensure its integrity over the design life, while Section B describes the RPV's role and construction as a critical pressure-retaining component, connecting them through the need for material integrity and longevity to meet the design life and embrittlement projections.

Section A describes the overall design and function of the NuScale reactor vessel, while Section B details the materials used in the reactor vessel, which are critical for assessing embrittlement projections and thus the vessel's ability to meet the intended design life.

Section A establishes that RCPB materials, including those of the RPV, comply with ASME BPVC requirements, while Section B provides specific details on how certain RPV welds and attachments meet those same ASME BPVC standards, thus Section B specifies compliance details for the general compliance statement in Section A.

Section A specifies the material standards and regulatory compliance for the RCPB, while Section B provides details on the shipment, handling, and storage requirements for the RPV, both referencing ASME BPVC standards to ensure long-term vessel integrity relevant to design life and embrittlement projections.

Section B provides embrittlement projections and material property analyses for the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which are necessary to determine whether the NuScale design life (from Ch.01) can be achieved without exceeding regulatory embrittlement limits.

Section A discusses the design life and integrity requirements for turbine components, while Section B details inspection and handling requirements for the reactor pressure vessel (RPV); both sections are connected through their focus on ensuring long-term structural integrity of critical plant components, which is essential for meeting the overall design life of the NuScale plant.

Section A describes the components and design basis of the NuScale reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B details the RPV's compliance with ASME code requirements for attachments and welds, which are relevant to the vessel's integrity and embrittlement projections over the design life.

Section A describes the components and design basis of the NuScale reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B outlines the inspection, shipment, and storage requirements for the RPV, with both sections connected through their focus on the RPV's integrity and compliance with regulatory standards over its design life.

Section A establishes the overall design life and regulatory conformance of the NuScale reactor, while Section B provides embrittlement projections and material analyses for the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which are critical for ensuring the RPV remains within safe operational limits throughout the design life specified in Section A.

Section A provides the overall NuScale design life as described in Ch.01, which sets the operational timeframe that must be supported by the RPV embrittlement projections detailed in Section B (Ch.05), thus the embrittlement analysis must demonstrate that the reactor pressure vessel remains within acceptable limits for the entire design life.

Section A describes the material standards and regulatory compliance for the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which directly affect the RPV's resistance to embrittlement over the design life discussed in Section B, where the RPV's integration with other components and its operational context are outlined.

Section A details the compliance of RCPB materials, including the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), with ASME and regulatory requirements relevant to design life, while Section B documents that certain fracture toughness criteria for Class 2 and 3 components are not applicable to NuScale, clarifying the regulatory scope for embrittlement projections.

Section A describes the components and design basis of the NuScale reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which is directly relevant to assessing its design life, while Section B documents regulatory conformance regarding material fracture toughness, which is a key factor in RPV embrittlement projections and thus impacts the evaluation of design life.

Section A discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure compliance with technical specifications related to reactor vessel integrity over the plant's design life, while Section B provides specific ASME code requirements and analyses for RPV attachments and welds, which are part of the technical basis for those specifications.

Section A discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure reactor vessel integrity over the design life, while Section B outlines inspection and handling requirements that support maintaining vessel integrity, thus both sections are connected through their roles in ensuring the RPV meets embrittlement and safety criteria throughout the design life.

Section B provides technical data and analyses on RPV embrittlement, which directly impacts the NuScale design life discussed in Section A, as embrittlement projections are a key factor in determining whether the design life can be achieved.

Section B provides details on the inspection, shipment, and storage requirements for the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which are necessary to ensure the RPV's integrity and embrittlement projections over the NuScale design life described in Section A.

Section B provides technical details and code compliance information about RPV materials and welds, which are relevant to assessing whether the NuScale design life discussed in Section A can be supported given embrittlement projections.

Section A (Ch.01) establishes the NuScale design life, which sets the operational timeframe that must be considered in Section B (Ch.05)'s RPV embrittlement projections to ensure the vessel remains within safety limits throughout the design life.

Section A discusses the design life and degradation mechanisms of reactor coolant system components, while Section B provides regulatory compliance and inspection requirements for reactor pressure vessel (RPV) attachments; both sections are related through their implications for the overall design life and integrity of the NuScale reactor, as RPV embrittlement projections (Section B) must be compatible with the intended design life described in Section A.

Section B outlines the inspection and handling requirements for the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which are necessary to ensure the RPV's integrity and embrittlement projections over the NuScale design life discussed in Section A.

Section A and Section B both reference conformance of the NuScale Gaseous Waste Management System with regulatory criteria, specifically citing acceptance criteria and their applicability, indicating that Section A provides a general overview while Section B specifies details about seismic design and quality group classification within the same regulatory context.

Section A discusses regulatory conformance and design life criteria for the NuScale system, while Section B provides operational experience and degradation mechanisms relevant to the reactor coolant system, connecting them through the need to ensure that projected embrittlement and wear mechanisms do not compromise the design life established in Section A.

Section A outlines the regulatory conformance and design criteria for waste management systems, while Section B discusses the observed degradation mechanisms and risk assessments for reactor coolant system components, with both sections providing evidence relevant to evaluating whether the NuScale design life (from Ch.01) is supported by the projected material performance and degradation (from Ch.05).
